parsed_review.summary:
CP00-234 is a descriptor-only closeout pack that completes the RP06.P09 independent review-gate scope (M06 Synthetic Fixture Set tail, M07 Test And Golden Case Set, M08 Hermes Evidence Packet, M09 Claude Review Packet, M10 Closeout And Next Handoff head) across five sections totaling 86 planned RP06 units, and hands off to CP00-235 / RP07.P00.M00.S01 without opening runtime. Independent read-only inspection confirms the pack is well-supported and the production-readiness posture is correctly conservative.

Verified: (1) Pack identity (pack_id CP00-234, risk C, unit_count 86, range RP06.P09.M06.S07-RP06.P09.M10.S10, program RP06) is consistent across the prompt baseline, registry DMS_CORE_CP234_PACK_BINDING, contracts/dms-core-contract.json current_pack, the live closeout-pack-plan.json entry (matching source_ledger_sha 13c7ed65...), and the review baseline. (2) Deliverable distribution (implementation 57, claude_review 12, security_audit 8, test 4, ui 4, hermes_evidence 1 = 86) and micro-title distribution (14/22/20/20/10 = 86) match the prompt and are enforced by validateDmsCoreCp234Coverage (validators.js:8070). (3) All seven required public exports exist (service.js:5976/6009, validators.js:8070/8105/8179/8200/8223) and are re-exported through index.js wildcard exports. (4) No-write/no-real-data boundaries are comprehensively enforced by freezeCp234Result (service.js:5925) and DMS_CORE_CP234_NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION (registry.js:1107): synthetic_only, no_real_data, no state writes/permission/audit runtime, opens_rp07_runtime=false, emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false. (5) Authority boundary is correct: the generated descriptor sets claude_is_final_approval=false, local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust=false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening=true; Claude tools are limited to Read/Grep/Glob; the attestation sets promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false and claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation=false. (6) The pack-level production_ready flag in command-evidence.json is false (status implementation_and_plan_gates_passed_pending_final_closeout); only production_ready_candidate=true (coverage.valid && closeout.valid) is asserted by the Hermes packet, and production_ready_flag is a named descriptor-verification marker, not a boolean enterprise-trust claim. No production_ready gate is weakened. (7) CP178+ review-hardening blockers are present in forbidden_review_evidence (auth_failure, stdout_0_bytes, malformed_json, fenced_json_plus_prose, tool_call_shaped_output, claude_final_approval_claim, source_mutation_claim, production_or_enterprise_trust_claim) and inherited by CP234; the readiness auth-preflight produced valid non-empty JSON. (8) Hermes control-plane usage is confined to deterministic descriptor-only review-packet/receipt handling (hermes_packet receipt_shape descriptor_only_p09_review_closeout_no_write); hermes_runtime_embedded_in_product=false. (9) Tests (model.test.js:2980-3030) are substantive and validate against the real plan pack and contract projection; the validation script (validate-rp06-dms-core-contract.mjs) asserts coverage/closeout validity, descriptor-only rows, authority boundary, no-write attestation, and preserves all prior-pack (CP198-CP233) coverage as a regression guard. (10) The contract p09_review_closeout_descriptor projection faithfully mirrors the generated descriptor; README documents the pack at #cp00-234; changed-file scope is clean and unrelated dirty files are correctly segregated. No P0 or P1 issues found. One P3 clarity note on review-harness metadata naming, which is non-blocking and does not affect the product descriptor's correct deferral of enterprise trust to human approval. Final enterprise-trust/production approval remains a human gate outside this read-only review, consistent with the authority boundary.

findings.title: Review-harness authority_boundary field 'enterprise_trust_claim_closed: true' is ambiguous in isolation
findings.details: The review baseline's authority_boundary (also echoed in prompt.txt and readiness/request artifacts) contains both 'human_final_approval_required_for_enterprise_trust_claim': true and 'enterprise_trust_claim_closed': true. Read in isolation, 'enterprise_trust_claim_closed: true' could be misread as asserting that enterprise trust has been achieved by this automated descriptor-only pack, which would contradict the required human-approval gate and the standing directive to never weaken production_ready gates. In context this is benign: review-packet.md clarifies it means 'Human/final approval and enterprise trust claim: closed outside this receipt' (i.e. handled outside this receipt's scope, not asserted here), and the product-side descriptor (service.js:6021 authority_boundary) unambiguously sets local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust=false with human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening=true. The product descriptor does not emit any 'enterprise_trust_claim_closed' field, so there is no product-level overclaim. This is purely a naming-clarity issue in the review-harness metadata.
findings.recommendation: Rename the metadata field to something self-documenting (e.g. 'enterprise_trust_claim_handled_outside_receipt' or 'enterprise_trust_claim_in_scope_of_this_receipt': false) or pair it with an inline gloss, so the harness metadata cannot be misread as an automated enterprise-trust closure independent of the documented human-approval gate.
findings.post_review_disposition: Non-blocking. Substance verified correct: enterprise trust is deferred to human final approval and the product descriptor sets local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust=false. No code or gate change required for CP00-234 closeout; recommend a wording adjustment in a future review-hardening pass.
